betterquesting:10.desc:8:
The §6Hot Turf§f (advancedrocketry:hotturf) is a §6single block§f world-gen block that spawns on §6hot, volcanic planets§f in the AR progression — Mercury, Venus-like worlds, and lava planets. The block has a §6red-orange oxidized appearance§f and is the §6signature terrain block§f of high-temperature planetary missions.

§6Production Path (world generation)§f:
- Source: §6Hot Turf§f is generated by AR's planet dimension system on hot planets
- It cannot be crafted — it must be §6mined from a hot planet§f during a landing mission
- The block's full in-game name is §6Oxidized Ferric Sand§f (tile.hotDryturf.name in en_US.lang)
- Drops: 1x §6Hot Turf§f when mined with any pickaxe (will §6burn the player§f if not wearing a §6Space Suit§f with thermal protection)

The block is §6purely cosmetic§f in its base form but is the §6prerequisite§f for the §6Basalt§f (10075) and §6Geode§f (10076) chains. The quest requires the player to mine §6Hot Turf§f and bring it back to the home planet.

This quest is a prerequisite for the §6Basalt§f (10075) and §6Geode§f (10076) — both of which are §6volcanic planet materials§f. The questline teaches the player that §6different planets§f have §6unique resources§f and that §6specialized missions§f are required to obtain them.

§cWarning§f: Hot planets are typically §6+200°C surface temperature§f. Without a §6Space Suit§f (10049) with thermal protection, the player will take §62 HP per second§f from heat damage. The §6Space Suit§f's §6Monopropellant Fuel Tank§f is consumed at §62× rate§f in hot environments.

§eTip§f: Plan a §6multi-day mission§f to a hot planet because the §6landing + mining + return trip§f takes at least 30 minutes of real-time. Pair with a §6Fueling Station§f (10025) on the home planet and a §6Gas Charge Pad§f (10052) to keep the suit topped up.
